perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and to its representative whose participation is necessary or helpful to evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction and who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any third party without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: By changing Section 3 to allow disclosure to 'any third party without restriction', this fundamentally contradicts the original intent of the agreement and the non-disclosure obligation defined throughout, specially when we reach Section 4 about 'Nondisclosure of Possible Business Transaction'. This renders the confidentiality protections of the agreement ineffective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure of Possible Business Transaction . Except as permitted by the previous paragraph, neither the Receiving Party nor the Receiving Party’s representatives will disclose to any person the fact that the Confidential information has been made available to the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s representatives or that the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party’s representatives have inspected any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any third party without restriction." This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states "...neither the Receiving Party nor the Receiving Party’s representatives will disclose to any person the fact that the Confidential information..." because the former allows unrestricted disclosure while the latter prohibits disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Because of the unique and proprietary nature of Confidential Information, it is understood and agreed that remedies at law for a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this Agreement may be inadequate and that in the event of such breach or threatened breach the 1 Disclosing Party will be entitled to seek equitable relief, including without limitation injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: Because of the potential immateriality of Confidential Information, it is understood and agreed that remedies at law for a breach by the Receiving Party of its obligations under this Agreement will be adequate, and the Disclosing Party will not be entitled to seek equitable relief, including without limitation injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing Section 6 to state that remedies at law are adequate due to the potential 'immateriality' of confidential information directly contradicts the entire purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement. It also removes the right to seek equitable relief, weakening the Disclosing Party's ability to protect its information. This also makes any remedy to the mentioned breaches not viable, even if they are mentioned in Section 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Restrictions and Exceptions . The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and to its representative whose participation is necessary or helpful to evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction and who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Receiving Party is not prohibited from disclosing Confidential Information it is legally compelled to disclose (by deposition, subpoena, civil investigative demand, court order or otherwise), provided that the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice of any such required disclosure so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained and the Disclosing Party has not waived compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party is advised by opinion of counsel it is legally required to furnish and will exercise reasonable efforts to limit the scope and content of such disclosure and obtain confidential treatment of such Confidential Information, Furthermore, the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement do not apply with respect to any Confidential Information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than from the improper action of the Receiving Party, (b) is developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, (c) was in the Receiving Party’s possession before receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (d) is received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not subject to an obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that remedies at law will be adequate and that the Disclosing Party will not be entitled to seek equitable relief. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines disclosure restrictions and exceptions and implicitly assumes that the Disclosing Party *can* seek protective orders or other remedies, including equitable relief. The explanation provided also highlights this contradiction, stating that it weakens the Disclosing Party's ability to protect its information and makes remedies for breaches not viable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is not assignable by either party without the prior express written consent of the other, except that UHS may assign this Agreement to an affiliate or to a successor in interest to which the business relates.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is freely assignable by either party without the need for consent. There are no restrictions on assignment.
perturbation.explanation: By making the agreement freely assignable in Section 7, it removes the protections afforded to the Disclosing Party by controlling who has access to the Confidential Information. This directly conflicts with Section 3, as any 3rd party can be recipient of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Restrictions and Exceptions . The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and to its representative whose participation is necessary or helpful to evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction and who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Receiving Party is not prohibited from disclosing Confidential Information it is legally compelled to disclose (by deposition, subpoena, civil investigative demand, court order or otherwise), provided that the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice of any such required disclosure so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained and the Disclosing Party has not waived compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party is advised by opinion of counsel it is legally required to furnish and will exercise reasonable efforts to limit the scope and content of such disclosure and obtain confidential treatment of such Confidential Information, Furthermore, the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement do not apply with respect to any Confidential Information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than from the improper action of the Receiving Party, (b) is developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, (c) was in the Receiving Party’s possession before receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (d) is received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not subject to an obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts assignment of the agreement, while the changed text makes it freely assignable, removing any restrictions. The contradicted text restricts disclosure of confidential information to specific employees and representatives who have agreed to be bound by the agreement's terms. By allowing free assignment, the changed text allows confidential information to be shared with any third party, directly contradicting the disclosure restrictions outlined in the contradicted text. The explanation given further supports this.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party’s obligations imposed pursuant to this Agreement will continue for two years from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party’s obligations imposed pursuant to this Agreement will automatically terminate one month from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By reducing the term of the agreement to one month, it drastically reduces the period for which Confidential Information is protected. This renders the agreement ineffective and creates an in-text contradiction with the stated purpose and intention of the document. The contradiction can be seen in Section 3, since it says the business transaction has to be bound by the terms of the Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Restrictions and Exceptions . The Receiving Party will disclose Confidential Information only to its employees and to its representative whose participation is necessary or helpful to evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction and who have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will limit the unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own confidential information. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Receiving Party is not prohibited from disclosing Confidential Information it is legally compelled to disclose (by deposition, subpoena, civil investigative demand, court order or otherwise), provided that the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice of any such required disclosure so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained and the Disclosing Party has not waived compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the Receiving Party is advised by opinion of counsel it is legally required to furnish and will exercise reasonable efforts to limit the scope and content of such disclosure and obtain confidential treatment of such Confidential Information, Furthermore, the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement do not apply with respect to any Confidential Information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than from the improper action of the Receiving Party, (b) is developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, (c) was in the Receiving Party’s possession before receipt from the Disclosing Party, or (d) is received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not subject to an obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly reduces the term of the agreement from two years to one month. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the Receiving Party's ongoing obligations regarding Confidential Information necessary to 'evaluate and advance to the Business Transaction' and the need to limit unauthorized disclosure for that unspecified duration. A one-month term is insufficient for the purpose, rendering the obligations outlined in the contradicted text ineffective and creating a clear in-text contradiction. The change means they would no longer be bound by the confidentiality clause after one month. This breaks the continuity expected by the contradicted text.
